title: Managing your food and fluid intake when on haemodialysis
summary: The document provides guidance for patients on haemodialysis regarding dietary adjustments. It emphasizes the need to alter food and fluid intake due to reduced kidney function. The document details specific dietary considerations for protein, salt, potassium, phosphate, and fibre, offering recommendations on food sources and alternatives. It also includes information on reducing salt intake through cooking methods and flavourings, lists high and lower salt alternatives, and explains how to understand food labelling for salt content. Finally, it discusses fluid intake, explaining the importance of managing fluid balance on haemodialysis and the signs of fluid overload.
